action.content: Write a funny poem for kids about a product that you would like to sell. The poem should have exactly 6 stanzas. Separated the stanzas using 3 asterisk markers: ***
action.label: context

action.content:
I have a gadget, shiny and new,
It’s the best invention that I ever knew.
It doesn’t need batteries, plugs, or wires,
It works on magic and silly desires.
It’s small enough to fit in your pocket,
But big enough to make your teacher look shocked.

***

It’s called the "Sock-Saver 3000,"
The perfect gift for everyone.
You know that feeling, deep in your heart,
When one sock vanishes, tearing you apart?
The dryer eats them, the cat hides them away,
This device brings them back to save the day.

***

Just press the button with a happy click,
And watch the laundry do a magic trick.
It scans the floorboards, checks under the bed,
And finds the missing sock, just like it said.
No more mismatched toes in the morning light,
No more wearing socks that don’t quite fit right.

***

It works on lefts, it works on rights,
It works on stripes and polka dots and plights.
If your sock is lost in the great beyond,
This little box will bring it back, unbound.
It even finds the ones you thought were gone,
Since before the dawn of the break of dawn.

***

But wait! There’s more to this clever plan,
It’s the most useful tool in all the land.
It doesn’t just find socks, oh no, not true,
It finds your lost remote and your old shoe.
It finds your homework, your keys, your pen,
It finds the things you can’t find again.

***

So buy one now, don’t wait a beat,
Make your life complete and sweet.
For the price of a cookie or two,
You’ll get the Sock-Saver, me and you.
No more crying, no more fuss,
Just happy feet and happy us!
action.label: response
